categories.name: صِفَاتٌ بَشَرِيَّةٌ سَلْبِيَّةٌ
categories.nameEn: Negative human traits mentioned in the Quran

items.verse: وَلَئِنْ أَذَقْنَا ٱلْإِنسَـٰنَ مِنَّا رَحْمَةًۭ ثُمَّ نَزَعْنَـٰهَا مِنْهُ إِنَّهُۥ لَيَـُٔوسٌۭ كَفُورٌۭ
items.verseEn: And if We give man a taste of mercy from Us and then We withdraw it from him, indeed, he is despairing and ungrateful.

items.verse: لَّا يَسْـَٔمُ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنُ مِن دُعَآءِ ٱلْخَيْرِ وَإِن مَّسَّهُ ٱلشَّرُّ فَيَـُٔوسٌۭ قَنُوطٌۭ
items.verseEn: Man is not weary of supplication for good [things], but if evil touches him, he is hopeless and despairing.

categories.name: صِفَاتُ الرِّيَاحِ فِي الْقُرْآنِ
categories.nameEn: Quranic adjectives describing types of wind

items.verse: فَأَرْسَلْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ رِيحًۭا صَرْصَرًۭا فِىٓ أَيَّامٍۢ نَّحِسَاتٍۢ لِّنُذِيقَهُمْ عَذَابَ ٱلْخِزْىِ فِى ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا ۖ وَلَعَذَابُ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ أَخْزَىٰ ۖ وَهُمْ لَا يُنصَرُونَ
items.verseEn: So We sent upon them a screaming wind during days of misfortune to make them taste the punishment of disgrace in the worldly life; but the punishment of the Hereafter is more disgracing, and they will not be helped.

items.verse: وَلِسُلَيْمَـٰنَ ٱلرِّيحَ عَاصِفَةًۭ تَجْرِى بِأَمْرِهِۦٓ إِلَى ٱلْأَرْضِ ٱلَّتِى بَـٰرَكْنَا فِيهَا ۚ وَكُنَّا بِكُلِّ شَىْءٍ عَـٰلِمِينَ
items.verseEn: And to Solomon [We subjected] the wind, blowing forcefully, proceeding by his command toward the land which We had blessed. And We are ever, of all things, Knowing.

items.verse: وَفِى عَادٍ إِذْ أَرْسَلْنَا عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلرِّيحَ ٱلْعَقِيمَ
items.verseEn: And in ʿAad [was a sign], when We sent against them the barren wind.

items.verse: أَمْ أَمِنتُمْ أَن يُعِيدَكُمْ فِيهِ تَارَةً أُخْرَىٰ فَيُرْسِلَ عَلَيْكُمْ قَاصِفًۭا مِّنَ ٱلرِّيحِ فَيُغْرِقَكُم بِمَا كَفَرْتُمْ ۙ ثُمَّ لَا تَجِدُوا۟ لَكُمْ عَلَيْنَا بِهِۦ تَبِيعًۭا
items.verseEn: Or do you feel secure that He will not send you back into it [i.e., the sea] another time and send upon you a hurricane of wind and drown you for what you denied? Then you would not find for yourselves against Us an avenger.

categories.name: أَدَوَاتُ ٱلِٱسْتِفْهَامِ ٱلْإِنْكَارِيِّ
categories.nameEn: Rhetorical question particles implying a negative answer

items.verse: أَفَلَا يَتَدَبَّرُونَ ٱلْقُرْءَانَ أَمْ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبٍ أَقْفَالُهَآ
items.verseEn: Then do they not reflect upon the Qur’ān, or are there locks upon [their] hearts?
Did We not expand for you, [O Muḥammad], your breast?

items.verse: أَلَيْسَ ٱللَّهُ بِكَافٍ عَبْدَهُۥ ۖ وَيُخَوِّفُونَكَ بِٱلَّذِينَ مِن دُونِهِۦ ۚ وَمَن يُضْلِلِ ٱللَّهُ فَمَا لَهُۥ مِنْ هَادٍۢ
items.verseEn: Is not Allāh sufficient for His Servant [i.e., Prophet Muḥammad (ﷺ)]? And [yet], they threaten you with those [they worship] other than Him. And whoever Allāh leaves astray - for him there is no guide.

items.verse: أَمَّنْ خَلَقَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ وَأَنزَلَ لَكُم مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءًۭ فَأَنۢبَتْنَا بِهِۦ حَدَآئِقَ ذَاتَ بَهْجَةٍۢ مَّا كَانَ لَكُمْ أَن تُنۢبِتُوا۟ شَجَرَهَآ ۗ أَءِلَـٰهٌۭ مَّعَ ٱللَّهِ ۚ بَلْ هُمْ قَوْمٌۭ يَعْدِلُونَ
items.verseEn: [More precisely], is He [not best] who created the heavens and the earth and sent down for you rain from the sky, causing to grow thereby gardens of joyful beauty which you could not [otherwise] have grown the trees thereof? Is there a deity with Allāh? [No], but they are a people who ascribe equals [to Him].

categories.name: أَصْوَاتُ الْفَزَعِ فِي الْآخِرَةِ
categories.nameEn: Nouns describing sounds of distress on the Day of Judgment and in Hellfire

items.verse: فَأَمَّا ٱلَّذِينَ شَقُوا۟ فَفِى ٱلنَّارِ لَهُمْ فِيهَا زَفِيرٌۭ وَشَهِيقٌ
items.verseEn: As for those who were [destined to be] wretched, they will be in the Fire. For them therein is [violent] exhaling and inhaling.

items.verse: إِذَآ أُلْقُوا۟ فِيهَا سَمِعُوا۟ لَهَا شَهِيقًۭا وَهِىَ تَفُورُ
items.verseEn: When they are thrown into it, they hear from it a [dreadful] inhaling while it boils up.

items.verse: وَإِن نَّشَأْ نُغْرِقْهُمْ فَلَا صَرِيخَ لَهُمْ وَلَا هُمْ يُنقَذُونَ
items.verseEn: And if We should will, We could drown them; then no one responding to a cry would there be for them, nor would they be saved

items.verse: وَيَـٰقَوْمِ إِنِّىٓ أَخَافُ عَلَيْكُمْ يَوْمَ ٱلتَّنَادِ
items.verseEn: And O my people, indeed I fear for you the Day of Calling -

harf.hint: Used to describe the finality of prophethood, or an object for authentication.
harf.arabicVerse: مَّا كَانَ مُحَمَّدٌ أَبَآ أَحَدٍۢ مِّن رِّجَالِكُمْ وَلَـٰكِن رَّسُولَ ٱللَّهِ وَخَاتَمَ ٱلنَّبِيِّـۧنَ ۗ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ بِكُلِّ شَىْءٍ عَلِيمًۭا
harf.verse: 33:40 — Muḥammad is not the father of [any] one of your men, but [he is] the Messenger of Allāh and seal [i.e., last] of the prophets. And ever is Allāh, of all things, Knowing.

deduction.title: The River's Secret
deduction.intro: In a time of great tyranny, when the cry of newborns was met with fear, a devoted parent was divinely inspired with a plan that defied all logic. It was an act of profound faith, entrusting a precious life to the currents of a mighty river, directly into the heart of the enemy's domain. Can you identify this soul whose trust in Allah's plan saved a future prophet?
clues: I lived under the rule of a tyrant who saw a threat in the birth of every baby boy among my people.
When my child was born, my heart was filled with fear, but Allah inspired me with a way to protect him.
I was instructed to place my infant son in a basket and cast him into the river, with a promise that he would be returned to me.
My heart felt empty after I let him go, and I almost revealed my secret out of grief, but Allah strengthened me.
I sent my daughter to follow the basket from a distance, to see what would become of my son.
When the household of Fir'awn found my son and he refused all wet nurses, I was brought to him and reunited with him, becoming his nurse without them knowing I was his mother.

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: The Wife of 'Imran
The Mother of Prophet Musa
The Wife of Prophet Zakariyya
The Wife of Prophet Ibrahim (Sarah)
Hajar, Mother of Isma'il

trial.label: What Was My Great Test?
options: Placing my infant son in the river
Making a vow to dedicate my child to God's service
Being accused of unchastity after a miraculous birth
Enduring barrenness until old age
Being left in a barren valley with my child
trial.answer: Placing my infant son in the river

location.label: What Was The Key Object?
options: A wooden ark
A colorful coat
A golden calf
A basket/chest
A clay bird

outcome.label: What Was The Outcome?
options: My son spoke from the cradle to defend my honor
My son was sold into slavery in a distant land
My son was returned to me to nurse
I was given glad tidings of a son by angels in old age
My son was nearly sacrificed and ransomed with a ram
outcome.answer: My son was returned to me to nurse

deduction.arabic: فَرَدَدْنَـٰهُ إِلَىٰٓ أُمِّهِۦ كَىْ تَقَرَّ عَيْنُهَا وَلَا تَحْزَنَ وَلِتَعْلَمَ أَنَّ وَعْدَ ٱللَّهِ حَقٌّۭ وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ
deduction.verse: So We restored him to his mother that she might be content and not grieve and that she would know that the promise of Allāh is true. But most of them [i.e., the people] do not know. (28:13)

translations: Both of them said Our Lord
and have mercy (on) us surely, we will be
scramble.hint: This is the prayer of repentance made by our first parents, Adam and Eve.
scramble.arabic: قَالَا رَبَّنَا ظَلَمْنَآ أَنفُسَنَا وَإِن لَّمْ تَغْفِرْ لَنَا وَتَرْحَمْنَا لَنَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلْخَـٰسِرِينَ
scramble.verseEn: They said, "Our Lord, we have wronged ourselves, and if You do not forgive us and have mercy upon us, we will surely be among the losers."

verse.arabic_text: مَّنْ عَمِلَ صَـٰلِحًا فَلِنَفْسِهِۦ ۖ وَمَنْ أَسَآءَ فَعَلَيْهَا ۖ ثُمَّ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكُمْ تُرْجَعُونَ
verse.translation: Whoever does a good deed—it is for their own soul; and whoever does evil—it is against it. Then to your Lord you will all be returned.

theme_question.correct: The principle of individual accountability for one's actions.
options: The principle of individual accountability for one's actions.
The necessity of collective responsibility for societal sins.
The power of intercession to erase all misdeeds.
The specific rewards for fasting during Ramadan.

educational_notes.verse_context: This verse appears in Surah Al-Jathiyah after a mention of the blessings and guidance given to the Children of Israel, who then differed among themselves. The verse serves as a universal and timeless principle, clarifying that regardless of one's community or lineage, personal actions are what truly matter. It establishes a direct link between one's deeds and their consequences.
educational_notes.theme_explanation: The core theme is one of the pillars of Islamic creed: ultimate individual accountability. It powerfully refutes any notion of inherited sin or vicarious atonement. Each soul is the sole bearer of its own burdens and the sole beneficiary of its own righteousness. The good one does benefits their own soul, and the evil one commits harms it. The verse concludes by reminding us that this system of personal responsibility culminates in our return to Allah for the final, perfect judgment.
educational_notes.surah_overview: Surah Al-Jathiyah is a Meccan surah whose name, 'The Kneeling,' is derived from verse 28, which vividly depicts every nation kneeling on the Day of Judgment to receive its record of deeds. The surah robustly defends the Quran's divine origin and the reality of the Resurrection. It directs the reader to contemplate Allah's signs in the universe—the heavens, the earth, the alternation of night and day—as undeniable proof of His power and wisdom. It strongly condemns the arrogance of those who mock these signs and prioritize their own desires over divine guidance.
